title: Structs 🏗️
description: Create custom data types by grouping multiple values.

slides.title: Beyond Tuples 📦
slides.content:
Tuples let us pack multiple values together... but relying strictly on index numbers like `.0` and `.1` gets confusing.

To create a robust custom data type with named fields, we use a **Struct**.

slides.title: Defining a Struct 📐
slides.content: You define a struct outside of your main function using the `struct` keyword, naming its fields and their types.

quizData.question: What is the primary advantage of a Struct over a Tuple?

options.text: Structs give meaningful names to each piece of data, rather than relying on index positions.

quizData.explanation: Structs allow you to explicitly name your data fields (`email`, `active`), making complex data infinitely easier to manage than `tuple.1` and `tuple.3`.

slides.title: Creating Instances 🧱
Accessing and Modifying 🔑

quizData.question: Can you declare a specific struct field (like `email`) as mutable while the rest of the struct is strictly immutable?
quizData.explanation: No, Rust requires that the *entire instance* must be mutable. You cannot mark individual fields of a struct as mutable. It's all or nothing!
